Title: Photoluminescence Properties of Lignin With a Genetically Introduced Luminophore in a Transgenic Hybrid Aspen That Overproduces Feruloyl-CoA 6′-Hydroxylase

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates feruloyl-CoA to form 6′-hydroxyferuloyl-CoA, a precursor in scopoletin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes scopoletin incorporation into lignin, enhancing lignin photoluminescence intensity, shifting emission to longer wavelengths, limiting quenching, and enabling reversible photodimerisation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides lignin with improved photoluminescent and photoresponsive material properties.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in transgenic xylem-producing stems; higher-overexpression lines accumulated more scopoletin and showed stronger phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis F6′H1 overexpression in hybrid aspen caused scopoletin accumulation and its incorporation into lignin. 2. Higher F6′H1 dosage increased photoluminescence intensity and red-shifted emission of cellulolytic enzyme lignin. 3. Scopoletin-modified lignin showed limited quenching in low-polarity solvents and at high concentration, clear fluorescence in films, and reversible coumarin-like photodimerisation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High-accumulation lines (#10 and #16) showed severe dwarfism; lignin content was slightly reduced in F6′H1 lines.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering woody biomass with scopoletin-modified lignin for photoluminescent and photoresponsive biomaterials; expression level should be tuned to balance functionality and growth penalty.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Solvent polarity; lignin concentration; aqueous pH; UV irradiation at 365 nm and 254 nm; polymer matrix (PMMA, PVA).
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Detected scopoletin in lignin after solvent extraction indicates polymer incorporation rather than free metabolite contamination; previous work cited indicates incorporation likely via ether linkages and improved saccharification efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in hybrid aspen; Py-GC/MS detection of lignin-incorporated scopoletin; UV-Vis/PL spectroscopy; SEC with fluorescence detection; photodimerisation assay.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: scopoletin/coumarin branch from feruloyl-CoA within phenylpropanoid-monolignol metabolism
